subject: English
title: Reading Quest
tagline: Read passages and answer comprehension questions to level up!

stages.name: Stage 1
stages.question: What is it called when you guess what happens next?
options: Reading quickly for the general idea
A statement that can be proven true
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Predicting.

stages.name: Stage 2
stages.question: What is the main idea of a passage called?
options: To convince the reader of an opinion
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Theme.

stages.name: Stage 3
stages.question: What do we call words that describe feelings in a text?
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Emotive language is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Emotive language.

stages.name: Stage 4
stages.question: What is 'skimming' a text?
options: Reading quickly to find specific information
Reading quickly for the general idea
A conclusion drawn from clues in the text
stages.correctOption: Reading quickly for the general idea
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Reading quickly for the general idea is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Reading quickly for the general idea.

stages.name: Stage 5
stages.question: What is 'scanning' a text?
options: Reading quickly to find specific information
stages.correctOption: Reading quickly to find specific information
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Reading quickly to find specific information is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Reading quickly to find specific information.

stages.name: Stage 6
stages.question: What is a summary?
options: The information around a word that helps explain its meaning
A short retelling of the main points
stages.correctOption: A short retelling of the main points
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A short retelling of the main points is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A short retelling of the main points.

stages.name: Stage 7
stages.question: What is an inference?
options: A conclusion drawn from clues in the text
The information around a word that helps explain its meaning
stages.correctOption: A conclusion drawn from clues in the text
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A conclusion drawn from clues in the text is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A conclusion drawn from clues in the text.

stages.name: Stage 8
stages.question: What is the author's purpose when they want to inform?
options: Reading quickly for the general idea
To convince the reader of an opinion
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! To give facts and information is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is To give facts and information.

stages.name: Stage 9
stages.question: What is the author's purpose when they want to persuade?
options: The information around a word that helps explain its meaning
A conclusion drawn from clues in the text
To convince the reader of an opinion
stages.correctOption: To convince the reader of an opinion
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! To convince the reader of an opinion is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is To convince the reader of an opinion.

stages.name: Stage 10
stages.question: What is a fact?
options: A statement that can be proven true
stages.correctOption: A statement that can be proven true
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A statement that can be proven true is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A statement that can be proven true.

stages.name: Stage 11
stages.question: What is an opinion?
options: The information around a word that helps explain its meaning
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A personal belief or view is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A personal belief or view.

stages.name: Stage 12
stages.question: What is context in reading?
options: The information around a word that helps explain its meaning
stages.correctOption: The information around a word that helps explain its meaning
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The information around a word that helps explain its meaning is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The information around a word that helps explain its meaning.

stages.name: Stage 13
stages.question: What do we call the sequence of events in a story?
options: The information around a word that helps explain its meaning
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Plot.

stages.name: Stage 14
stages.question: What is a 'cause' in a cause-and-effect passage?
options: A statement that can be proven true
Reading quickly for the general idea
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The reason something happens is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The reason something happens.

stages.name: Stage 15
stages.question: What is an 'effect' in a cause-and-effect passage?
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The result of what happens is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The result of what happens.
